questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the focus of a hyperbola is <math><mo>(</mo><mo>±</mo><mn>3,0</mn><mo>)</mo></math> and the equation of a tangent is <math><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the equation of the hyperbola is
questions.solution:
Given, ±ae, 0=±3, 0

⇒  ae=3

⇒ a2e2=9 

⇒ b2+a2=9 ...(i)

∵ 2x+y-4=0

⇒   y=-2x+4

is the tangent to the hyperbola

∴  42=a2-22-b2 ∵c2=a2m2-b2

⇒  4a2-b2=16 ...(ii)

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get,

a2=5, b2=4 

∴ Equation of hyperbola is x25–y24 =1

⇒ 4x2-5y2=20
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If m is a variable, the locus of the point of intersection of the lines <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>3</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>-</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>y</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>=</mo> <mtext>m</mtext> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>3</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>+</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mtext>y</mtext> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>=</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mtext>m</mtext> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math> is a/an :
questions.solution:
The required locus is obtained by eliminating the variable m from the given equations of the lines, then we have

x 3 - y 2 x 3 + y 2 = m 1 m

⇒   x 2 9 - y 2 4 = 1

This is clearly a hyperbola.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two straight lines having variable slopes <math><msub><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> pass through the fixed points <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> respectively. If <math><msub><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math>, then the eccentricity of the locus of the point of intersection of the lines is
questions.solution:
Let, the lines are y=m1x-a and y=m2x+a

which intersects at the point h,k.

⇒k=m1h-a &  k=m2h+a

⇒k2=m1m2h2-a2

⇒k22=h2-a2 (given, m1m2=2)

⇒x21-y22=a2 (Hyperbola)

Eccentricity =1+21=3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the focii of the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{25}+\frac{y^2}{16}=1$ and the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{4}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ coincide, then $b^2$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, equation of ellipse is
$$
\frac{x^2}{25}+\frac{y^2}{16}=1
$$
and equation of hyperbola is
$$
\frac{x^2}{4}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1
$$
eccentricity of ellipse
$$
\begin{aligned}
& b^2=a^2\left(1-e^2\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 16=25\left(1-e^2\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad e^2=1-\frac{16}{25}=\frac{9}{25} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad e= \pm \frac{3}{5} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Focii of the ellipse $=( \pm a e, 0)=( \pm 3,0)$ which coincide with focii of the hyperbola.
Let $e_1$ be the eccentricity of the hyperbola.
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & \pm a e_1= \pm 3 \\
\Rightarrow & e_1=\frac{3}{2}
\end{array}
$$
Now, $\quad b^2=a^2\left(e_1^2-1\right)$
$$
\begin{array}{cc}
\Rightarrow & b^2=4\left(\frac{9}{4}-1\right)=4 \times \frac{5}{4} \\
\Rightarrow & b^2=5
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x=9$ is a chord of contact of the hyperbola $x^2-y^2=9$, then the equation of the tangent at one of the points of contact is
questions.solution:
Given that, $x=9$ is a chord of contact of hyperbola.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2-y^2=9 \\
& \text { put } x=9, \quad 81-y^2=9 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad y^2=72 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad y=6 \sqrt{2} \text { or }-6 \sqrt{2} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Points are $(9,6 \sqrt{2})$ and $(9,-6 \sqrt{2})$
Now, differentiating Eq. (i) w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 x-2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x}{y} \\
& \text { at }(9,6 \sqrt{2}) \quad\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(9,6 \sqrt{2})}=\frac{9}{6 \sqrt{2}}=\frac{3}{2 \sqrt{2}} \\
& \text { and at }(9-6 \sqrt{2})\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(9,-6 \sqrt{2})}=-\frac{3}{2 \sqrt{2}} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Equation of tangent at $(9,6 \sqrt{2})$ is
$$
\begin{aligned}
(y-6 \sqrt{2}) & =\frac{3}{2 \sqrt{2}}(x-9) \\
\Rightarrow \quad 2 \sqrt{2} y-24 & =3 x-27 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 3 x-2 \sqrt{2} y-3 & =0
\end{aligned}
$$
and equation of tangent at $(9,-6 \sqrt{2})$ is
$$
\begin{aligned}
(y+6 \sqrt{2}) & =\frac{-3}{2 \sqrt{2}}(x-9) \\
\Rightarrow \quad 2 \sqrt{2} y+24 & =-3 x+27 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 3 x+2 \sqrt{2} y-3 & =0
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A hyperbola passing through a focus of the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{169}+\frac{y^2}{25}=1$. Its transverse and conjugate axes coincide respectively with the major and minor axes of the ellipse. The product of eccentricities is 1 . Then, the equation of the hyperbola is
questions.solution:
Let the equation of hyperbola be
$$
\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1
$$
Given equation of ellipse is
$$
\frac{x^2}{(13)^2}+\frac{y^2}{(5)^2}=1
$$
Here,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Here, } \quad a=13, b=5 \\
& \therefore \quad e=\sqrt{1-\frac{b^2}{a^2}} \\
& \therefore \quad=\sqrt{1-\frac{25}{169}}=\sqrt{\frac{144}{169}}=\frac{12}{13} \\
& \therefore \text { Focus }( \pm a e, 0)=\left( \pm 13 \times \frac{12}{13}, 0\right) \\
& \quad=( \pm 12,0)
\end{aligned}
$$
Since, Eq. (i) passes through $( \pm 12,0)$.
$$
\begin{array}{rrrl}
\therefore & \frac{144}{a^2}-\frac{0}{b^2}=1 \\
\Rightarrow & a^2=144 \\
\Rightarrow & a= \pm 12
\end{array}
$$
Now eccentricity of hyperbola
$$
\begin{aligned}
e^{\prime} & =\sqrt{1+\frac{b^2}{a^2}} \\
& =\sqrt{1+\frac{b^2}{144}}
\end{aligned}
$$
According to the equation,
$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& e^{\prime}=1 \\
\Rightarrow & & \frac{12}{13} \times \sqrt{1+\frac{b^2}{144}} & =1 \\
\Rightarrow & & \sqrt{1+\frac{b^2}{144}} & =\frac{13}{12} \\
\Rightarrow & & 1+\frac{b^2}{144} & =\frac{169}{144} \\
\Rightarrow & & \frac{b^2}{144} & =\frac{169}{144}-1 \\
\Rightarrow & & \frac{b^2}{144} & =\frac{25}{144} \\
b^2 & =25
\end{array}
$$
$\therefore$ Equation of hyperbola is
$$
\frac{x^2}{144}-\frac{y^2}{25}=1
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The mid point of the chord $4 x-3 y=5$ of the hyperbola $2 x^2-3 y^2=12$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $4 x-3 y=5$ and $2 x^2-3 y^2=12$
$\therefore \quad 2\left(\frac{5+3 y}{4}\right)^2-3 y^2=12$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{\left(25+9 y^2+30 y\right)}{8}-3 y^2=12 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 15 y^2-30 y+71=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\frac{30 \pm \sqrt{900-4260}}{30} \\ & =1 \pm \frac{\sqrt{-3360}}{30} \\ & \end{aligned}$
Also,
$\begin{aligned}
& 2 x^2-3\left(\frac{4 x-5}{3}\right)^2=12 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 10 x^2-40 x+61=0 \\
&
\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{40 \pm \sqrt{1600-4 \times 10 \times 61}}{2 \times 10}$
$=\frac{40 \pm \sqrt{-840}}{20}=2 \pm \frac{\sqrt{-840}}{20}$
$\therefore \quad$ Points are $A\left(2+\frac{\sqrt{-840}}{20}, 1+\frac{\sqrt{-3360}}{30}\right)$ and
$B\left(2-\frac{\sqrt{-840}}{20}, 1-\frac{\sqrt{-3360}}{30}\right) \text {. }$
$\therefore \quad$ Mid point of $A B$ is $(2,1)$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the hyperbola with vertices <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mfenced><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and semi-latus rectum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> is given by:
questions.solution:
We have a=3 and b2a=4⇒b2=12

Hence, the equation of the hyperbola is

x29−y212=1

⇒4x2−3y2=36

⇒ 4x2−3y2−36=0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the circle $x^2+y^2=a^2$ intersects the hyperbola $x y=c^2$ in four points $\left(x_i, y_i\right)$, for $i=1,2,3$ and 4 , then $y_1+y_2+y_3+y_4$ equals
questions.solution:
Given,
$\begin{array}{rlrl}
\text { Given, } & & x^2 y^2 & =c^4 \\
\Rightarrow & y^2\left(a^2-y^2\right) & =c^4 \\
\Rightarrow & y^4-a^2 y^2+c^4 & =0
\end{array}$
Let $y_1, y_2, y_3$ and $y_4$ are the roots.
$\therefore \quad y_1+y_2+y_3+y_4=0$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><msub><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> are the eccentricities of the ellipse <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>18</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and the hyperbola <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>9</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> respectively and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><msub><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is a point on the ellipse <math><mn>15</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>k</mi></math> , then the value of <math><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
e1=1-418=79=73
e1=1+49=139=133

Also, 
15e12+3e22=k⇒k=1579+3139
∴k=16
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the hyperbola $x^2-y^2-4 x+2 y+c=0$, if the focus is $S(2+2 \sqrt{2}, k)$ and the directrix that is adjacent to $S$ is $x=2+\sqrt{2}$, then $c=$
questions.solution:
Given hyperbola
$\begin{aligned}
x^2-y^2-4 x+2 y+c & =0 \\
(x-2)^2-(y-1)^2 & =3-c \\
\frac{(x-2)^2}{3-c}-\frac{(y-1)^2}{3-c} & =1
\end{aligned}$
Focus of hyperbola is
$\begin{aligned}
x-2 & = \pm \sqrt{(3-c)+(3-c)} \\
x-2 & = \pm \sqrt{6-2 c} \quad[\because a e=\sqrt{(3-c)+(3-c)}] \\
x & =2 \pm \sqrt{6-2 c}
\end{aligned}$
Here, $2+\sqrt{6-2 c}=2+2 \sqrt{2}$
$\therefore \quad \sqrt{6-2 c}=2 \sqrt{2} \Rightarrow 6-2 c=8 \Rightarrow c=-1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the latus rectum of a hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> subtends an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> at the other focus, then the eccentricity of the hyperbola is
questions.solution:
Given that the latus rectum of a hyperbola x2a2-y2b2=1................i

subtends an angle of 60° at the other focus.

As, foci are Sae,0 & S'-ae,0

End Points of latus rectum through S are given Aae, b2a, Bae, -b2a

Given, AB subtends 600 at S'

Angle between S'A with x-axis will be 300.

Slope of S'A=m1=b22a2e=tan30°

⇒b22a2e=tan300=13

⇒b2a2=2e3........ii

Also, we know that

b2=a2e2-1

⇒b2a2=e2-1

Putting value of  b2a2 from ii

⇒e2-1=2e3

⇒3e2-2e-3=0

⇒3e2-3e+e-3=0

⇒e-33e+1=0

∴e=3,-13

But eccentricity can not be negative.

Hence, value of eccentricity e=3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The line $2 x+y=1$ is a tangent to the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1(a>b)$. If this line passes through the point of intersection of a directrix and the positive $X$-axis, then the eccentricity of that hyperbola is
questions.solution:
The line $2 x+y-1=0$ is a tangent to the hyperbola
$\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ and also passes through the point of intersection of directrix and the positive $x$-axis
Equation of direction $x=\frac{a}{e}$
$\therefore$ Point $\left(\frac{a}{e}, 0\right)$ passes through $2 x+y=1$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2\left(\frac{a}{e}\right)+0=1$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 a=e$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 a^2=a e$
We know, $(a e)^2=a^2+b^2$
$\therefore \quad 4 a^4=a^2+b^2$
Equation of tangent of $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$
is $y=m x \pm \sqrt{a^2 m^2-b^2}$
$\therefore \quad a^2(2)^2-b^2=1$

$$
\Rightarrow \quad 4 a^2-b^2=1
$$
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$$
\begin{array}{ll} 
& 4 a^4=a^2+4 a^2-1 \\
\Rightarrow & 4 a^4-5 a^2+1=0 \\
& \left(4 a^2-1\right)\left(a^2-1\right)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & a^2=1, \frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow a=1, \frac{1}{2} \\
\therefore & e=2 a \\
& e=2(1)=2
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mfenced><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced></math> be two points on the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> be the focus of the hyperbola. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi></math> are collinear and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Plotting the diagram of given data we get,

We know that equation of chord joining Aθ1 & Bθ2 is given by,

xacosθ1-θ22-ybsinθ1+θ22=cosθ1+θ22

Now given this chord is passing through focus ae,0 then,

aeacosθ1-θ22=cosθ1+θ22

⇒ecosθ1-θ22=cosθ1+θ22

⇒aa2+b2cosθ1-θ22=cosθ1+θ22

⇒acosθ1-θ22=a2+b2cosθ1+θ22

Now comparing with acosθ1-θ22=kcosθ1+θ22

We get ⇒k=a2+b2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of a point which moves such that the difference of its distances from the points <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>5,0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5,0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math><mn>6</mn></math> units is a conic, whose length of the latus rectum (in units) is equal to
questions.solution:
Because 6<5--5
Hence, the locus is a hyperbola by definition.
Let's assume the eccentricity, semi-transverse axis and the semi-conjugate axis of the hyperbola is e,a, and b respectively, then

2ae=10 {Distance between focii -5,0 & 5,0=10=2ae }
& 2a=6⇒e=106=53
⇒1+b2a2=e2=259⇒b2a2=259-1=169
⇒2b2a=2×169×a=323= length of the latus rectum
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the chord of the hyperbola <math><mn>25</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>16</mn><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>400</mn></math>, which is bisected at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
Given equation of hyperbola is 25x2-16y2=400.

If (6,2) is the mid-point of the chord, then equation of chord is T=S1.

⇒256x-162y-400=2536-16(4)-400

⇒75x-16y=418.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><msub><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is a point on the hyperbola <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>S</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>.</mo><msup><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>'</mi></mrow></msup><mi>P</mi><mo>=</mo></math> ________
questions.solution:
Given Hyperbola x2-y2=a2 is
Rectangular hyperbola, its e=2
Then S2a,0 and S'-2a,0
Also S'P=2x1+a and SP =2x1-a
Then SPS'P=2x12-a2∵x12-y12=a2
Hence, SPS'P=x12+y12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> lie on the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>(</mo><mi>a</mi><mo><</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, whose centre <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> be such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mi>P</mi></math> is perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mi>Q</mi></math>, then the value of <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi><msup><mrow><mi>P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>C</mi><msup><mrow><mi>Q</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
Let CP=r1 be inclined to transverse axis at an angle θ such that Pr1cos⁡θ, r1sin⁡θ lies on the hyperbola. Therefore,

r12 cos2⁡θa2-sin2⁡θb2=1

Replacing θ by 90o+θ, we get, 

r22 sin2⁡θa2-cos2⁡θb2=1

(where, CQ=r2)

⇒  1r12+1r22=cos2⁡θa2 -sin2⁡θb2+sin2⁡θa2-cos2⁡θb2

⇒  1r12+1r22=cos2⁡θ1a2-1b2+sin2⁡θ1a2-1b2

⇒  1r12+1r22=1a2-1b2

⇒  1CP2+1CQ2=1a2-1b2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $e_1$ is the eccentricity of the hyperbola $x=\sec \theta$, $y=\sqrt{2} \tan \theta$ and $e_2$ is the eccentricity of the hyperbola $x=\sqrt{2} \sec \theta, y=\tan \theta$, then $\frac{e_2^2}{e_1^2}=$
questions.solution:
Acc. to given parametric coordinates
$\mathrm{e}_1^2=\left(\sqrt{\frac{1^2+(\sqrt{2})^2}{1^2}}\right)^2$ and $\mathrm{e}_2^2\left(\sqrt{\frac{(\sqrt{2})^2+(1)^2}{(\sqrt{2})^2}}\right)^2$
Now $\frac{\mathrm{e}_2^2}{\mathrm{e}_1^2}=\frac{1}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of a point which is at a distance of 2 units from the line $2 x-3 y+4=0$ and at a distance of $\sqrt{13}$ units from a point $(5,0)$ is
questions.solution:
Let $P(h, k)$ be the point whose locus is to be find. given $\sqrt{(h-5)+(k-0)^2}=\sqrt{13}$
$$
\Rightarrow h^2=-k^2+10 h-12...(1)
$$
also given, $\frac{2 h-3 k+4}{\sqrt{4+9}}=2$
Squaring both sides, we get-
$$
\Rightarrow 4 h^2+9 k^2+16-12 h k-24 k+16 h=52
$$
Putting $h^2$ value in above equation we get
$$
\Rightarrow 12 h k-5 k^2-56 h+24 k+84=0
$$
for getting locus replacing $h, k$ by $x, y$ respectively
$$
\Rightarrow 12 x y-5 y^2-56 x+24 y+84=0
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of a variable point whose chord of contact w.r.t. the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> subtends a right angle at the origin is
questions.solution:
Given,

The equation of the given hyperbola is x2a2-y2b2=1     ⋯1

Now let h,k be the pole of a chord PQ of the hyperbola

Then the straight line PQ is the polar of the point h,k w.r.t. the hyperbola and so its equation is

xha2-ykb2=1      ⋯2

Now the center of the hyperbola is the origin, let it be point C,

Now making 1 homogeneous with the help of 2, the combined equation of CP and CQ is

x2a2-y2b2=xha2-ykb22     ⋯3

Now the chord PQ subtends a right angle at 0,0

Therefore the lines CP and CQ given by 3 are perpendicular and so in the equation 3, we have the coefficient of x2+coefficient of y2=0

⇒1a2-h2a4+-1b2-k2b4=0

⇒h2a4+k2b4=1a2-1b2

Thus locus is

x2a4+y2b4=1a2-1b2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If equation \(a x^2+2 h x y+b y^2+2 g x+2 f y+c=0\) represents a rectangular hyperbola, then
questions.solution:
It is fundamental concept that for equation
\(a x^2+2 h x y+b y^2+2 g x+2 f y+c=0\) to represents a rectangular hyperbola it has to satisfy following condition,
\(\Delta=\mathrm{abc}+2 \mathrm{fgh}-\mathrm{af}^2-\mathrm{bg}^2-\mathrm{ch}^2 \neq 0, \mathrm{h}^2>\mathrm{ab}\) and \(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}=0\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Locus of the middle points of the chords of the hyperbola <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mn>1</mn></math> which passes through a fixed point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is hyperbola whose centre is -
questions.solution:
T=S1 at mid point h, k hxa2-kyb2=h2a2- k2b2

pass α, β point

∴ hαa2 – kyb2=h2a2 – k2b2

∴ locus is x2-αxa2- y2-βyb2=0

⇒x-α/22 a2- y-β/22b2=14 α2a2-β2b2

= k2 let

is a hyperbola.

Where centre is α/2, β/2 .

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: From a point on the line <math><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> tangents are drawn to the hyperbola <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> such that the chord of contact passes through a fixed point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>μ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then, <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>λ</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let the point be α,β⇒β=α+2
Chord of contact of the hyperbola is T=0
⇒αx6-βy2=1
⇒αx6-α+2y2=1
⇒αx6-y2-2y2+1=0
Since, this passes through the fixed point λ,μ
∴λ6-μ2=0 and 2μ2+1=0

So, μ=-1 and λ=-3
⇒μ-λ=2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>25</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and the hyperbola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>25</mn></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> have ______ in common.
questions.solution:
For ellipse x225+y216=1, a=5 and b=4

Center =0, 0, vertices ±5, 0
 For hyperbola x225-y216=1, a=5 and b=4

Center 0, 0, vertices ±5, 0

For ellipse a2e2=a2-b2=25-16=9

For hyperbola 

a2e2=a2+b2=25+16=41.

∴ Only center and vertices are common.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: A normal to the hyperbola <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mn>1</mn></math> meets the axis in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> the line <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> are drawn at Right angle to the axis and meet at <math><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> then locus of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> is -
questions.solution:
Normal to the hyperbola is

ax cos⁡θ +by cot⁡θ =a2+b2

Putting y=0 and x=0

We get Aa2+b2asec⁡θ,0, B 0,a2+b2btan⁡θ

line through A , perpendicular to the x -axis is x =a2+b2asec⁡θ 

line through B , perpendicular to the y -axis is y =a2+b2btan⁡θ  

So intersection of these point is L

∵ sec2⁡θ - tan2⁡θ =1

⇒ a2x2- b2y2=a2+b22 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If one of the roots of the equation $x^2-5 x-14=0$ is the length of the semi conjugate axis of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ and the square of the other root is the semi-transverse axis then the focus of the hyperbola that lies on the positive $x$-axis is
questions.solution:
Given equation is $x^2-5 x-14=0$
how roots are $-2,7$
Let $b=7$ and $a=4$
hence $C=\sqrt{7^2+9^2}=\sqrt{65}$
hence $S=(\sqrt{65}, 0)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of middle points of chords of hyperbola <math><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> parallel to <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Let (h,k) is mid point of chord.

Then, its equation is T=S1

∴3hx-2ky+2x+h-3y+k=3h2-2k2+4h-6k

x3h+2+y-2k-3=3h2-2k2+2h-3k

Since, this line is parallel to y=2x3h+22k+3 =2⇒ 3h-4k=4

Thus, locus of point is 3x-4y=4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
